Metadata.title: TunixLabs — One operator, shipping production systems from Chile
Metadata.description: Not a studio. One operator with 15 years of operations leadership and MIT Professional Education ML training, shipping AI-powered systems for regulated industries. US time zones, English-fluent, production-ready.

hero.description: I build the systems that replace spreadsheets, paper, and manual chaos in regulated industries. 15 years running real operations before writing code. MSc Finance + MIT AI/ML. Based in Chile, US time zones, fluent English. Nearshore engagements at 3-5x below SF/NYC rates.

caseStudies.title: Production systems, not prototypes
caseStudies.subtitle: Software someone opens every morning at 7am. Real users, real numbers, real audit trails.
caseStudies.disclaimer: TunixLabs acts as technical partner for selected firms. Client names in these case studies refer to the direct commissioning partner, not the end-user of the software.

sime.client: Contractors at Chile's largest copper mining operations
sime.problem: Thousands of maintenance work orders on paper between field and office. No audit trail, no digital traceability.
sime.solution: Complete digital replacement: field workers sign with QR on-site. Supervisors approve from mobile. Automatic audit by shift cycle.
sime.metric: 4,000 digital route sheets per day · Zero paper in the field

schwager.problem: Field workers in gloves and rain can't fill safety forms on a phone. Paper forms get lost before reaching audit.
schwager.solution: Voice AI: workers complete safety forms by talking. No typing, no signal dependency. Legal signatures via email, WhatsApp, or web.
schwager.metric: 195+ workers speak instead of type · Sub-100ms responses

gasDistribution.client: Regional gas distribution operator in Chile
gasDistribution.problem: 14 delivery drivers losing 2 hours daily hand-typing discount codes. Typos mean failed deliveries and angry customers.
gasDistribution.solution: WhatsApp bot: driver sends photo of the code, AI reads it, burns it automatically. Driver stays on the road.
gasDistribution.metric: From 5 min per code to 30 seconds · 14 drivers back on route

apoderapp.problem: School treasurers collecting fees via spreadsheets and WhatsApp photos. No receipts, no transparency, no audit trail.
apoderapp.solution: WhatsApp-first platform: parents send payment photo, AI reads and verifies it automatically. Treasurer approves with one tap. Public transparency reports.
apoderapp.metric: School fee collection without spreadsheets · AI-verified receipts

fernandezMetallurgic.problem: A 50-year-old workshop running everything on Excel: HR, finance, projects, CRM. Owners traveled and nobody knew the real state of the business.
fernandezMetallurgic.solution: Custom ERP replacing every spreadsheet. OCR reads invoices, QR tracks tools, AI extracts data from floor documents. One system for the entire company.
fernandezMetallurgic.metric: 50 years of Excel replaced · One system, one source of truth

sgmSchwager.label: Voice AI for mining field ops
sgmSchwager.metric: Workers fill safety forms by talking. In gloves and rain.

apoderappDark.label: WhatsApp-first school fee collection
apoderappDark.metric: Treasurers collect fees without spreadsheets. AI verifies receipts.

fernandezErp.label: Custom ERP for industrial workshop
fernandezErp.metric: 50 years of Excel replaced. One system, entire company.

speakly.label: AI English learning academy
speakly.metric: Practice with AI avatar + human teacher with AI copilot

gasDistribution.label: Delivery driver automation bot
gasDistribution.metric: Photo of code via WhatsApp → auto-burned in 30 seconds

about.title: One person. Seven production systems.
about.name: Alejandro Moyano Foncea
about.roleLine: MSc Finance · MIT Professional Education AI/ML · 15 years ops before code
about.bioParagraph1: Not a studio. Not an agency. I'm one person who understands the business because I've been running them for 15 years — copper mines, gas distributors, industrial workshops — and now I build the systems that replace Excel, paper, and manual chaos. <mit>MIT Professional Education</mit> in AI/ML, MSc Finance, and agentic workflows on Claude Code.
about.bioParagraph2: I understand the *why* behind software, not just the *how*. Every system I ship solves a problem I've seen firsthand: workers who can't type with gloves, treasurers drowning in receipts, 50-year-old shops with no visibility into their own numbers. US time zone overlap, fluent English (California high school), rates 3-5x below SF/NYC — without giving up senior ownership.

credentials.caption: Alejandro Moyano Foncea · No Code AI and Machine Learning: Building Data Science Solutions · MIT Professional Education · Feb–May 2024 · 80 hours of effort
credentials.altText: MIT Professional Education credential issued to Alejandro Moyano Foncea

services.title: What I build
services.subtitle: Seven practice areas, each anchored to a real production project. No buzzwords, no invented stacks.

aiAssistants.title: Conversational assistants & Voice AI
aiAssistants.shortDescription: Voice agents and chatbots powered by frontier LLMs, not rigid flows. Running in the field today, not in a demo.
aiAssistants.longDescription: I build conversational assistants for real operations: field Voice AI so workers can complete safety forms hands-free — in gloves, rain and vibration — WhatsApp chatbots for SaaS product support, and Claude agents integrated directly into your backend. No Dialogflow, no Rasa: frontier LLMs (Claude, Google Gemini) orchestrated on top of your existing stack.
aiAssistants.anchorProject: In production: field Voice AI for a mining services contractor — 195+ field workers across 7 maintenance teams, 4,000+ daily route sheets, response times under 100ms.

businessIntelligence.title: Operational BI and dashboards
businessIntelligence.shortDescription: Dashboards ops directors actually open. Real data, real numbers, zero placeholders.
businessIntelligence.longDescription: I build operational dashboards and ETL pipelines for teams that need to see their operation's reality before morning coffee. Standard mining KPIs out of the box — OEE, MTBF, MTTR, Availability, CAUE, plan compliance, ABC criticality matrices — plus multi-year planning views with Gantt timelines and historical comparisons. Built for planners who live in SAP, not for pitch decks.
businessIntelligence.anchorProject: In production: BI platforms tracking 115K+ activities, 1,056 inspection protocols and 19,778 work orders for contractors at Chile's largest copper mining operations; audit dashboards tracking 103+ safety standards for an auditing firm serving major mining operators; multi-year planning views built directly from raw SAP data.

webDev.title: Web development & vertical SaaS
webDev.shortDescription: Full SaaS platforms: backend, frontend, integrations, CI/CD and deploy. Specialized in regulated industries.
webDev.longDescription: I build vertical SaaS end-to-end. Not marketing landings — the actual product: auth, billing, admin panels, integrations with legacy systems and government APIs (Chilean SII). Strict TypeScript, real testing, automated deploy. Code written to pass audits, not to impress investors.
webDev.anchorProject: In production: Apoderapp (Chilean SII tax compliance, sii-factura library with 88+ tests), Speakly (own AI English-learning platform on Turborepo), a custom AI-powered ERP for a 50-year-old metallurgic company, and the site you're reading right now.

aiConsulting.title: AI consulting & adversarial audits
aiConsulting.shortDescription: I audit your existing AI architecture, find hidden scope drift and propose the cheapest approach that actually works.
aiConsulting.longDescription: Adversarial audits of deployed AI systems: how solid is your pipeline, where is the hidden tech debt, when does an LLM make sense and when does a classical model win. I also design multi-agent architectures from scratch for clients who already have data and a clearly-scoped problem. The honest differentiator: 15 years of operations leadership before the first line of production code — I can tell the difference between a software problem and a process problem.
aiConsulting.anchorProject: Own experience: Agente_Tunix meta-system that orchestrates 7 production projects via hierarchical Claude agents, each with its own closing pipeline (type_check → lint → build → test → audit) and automated PR review.

rpa.title: Process automation (RPA with AI)
rpa.shortDescription: I automate repetitive processes with code, not with expensive RPA suites. Direct integration with your APIs.
rpa.longDescription: When your team burns hours processing Excel files, validating codes by hand or re-sending emails, I write a bot that does it in seconds. No UiPath, no Power Automate: a Node or Python service that runs on your infra and integrates with your existing systems. Cheaper, more auditable, and no enterprise licensing lock-in.
rpa.anchorProject: In production: code processing bot for a regional gas distribution operator in Chile — 4,040 codes, 88.7% success rate, 14 delivery routes automated. Plus OCR + QR + LLM Vision document digitization pipelines on the shop floor of a 50-year-old metallurgic company.

machineLearning.title: Machine learning & applied data science
machineLearning.shortDescription: Classical ML plus modern LLM stack. Trained through MIT Professional Education on Dataiku DSS, then replicated in Python.
machineLearning.longDescription: Honest differentiator vs the AI consultancies that appeared post-ChatGPT: I have classical ML fundamentals (feature engineering, model selection, cross-validation) that predate the GPT boom, plus finance fundamentals from a finance MSc. If your problem is prediction on structured data, I won't push an LLM that hallucinates — I'll build a model that works and can be audited. Training was MIT Professional Education, 80 hours of effort on Dataiku DSS, Feb–May 2024.
machineLearning.anchorProject: Training: MIT Professional Education program "No Code AI and Machine Learning: Building Data Science Solutions" (80h, 2024) on Dataiku DSS, then replicated models in Python with Claude as coding assistant.

computerVision.title: Computer vision (capability in progress)
computerVision.shortDescription: Honest framing: I have the fundamentals and the interest, but no production CV projects of my own yet.
computerVision.longDescription: Honest section: computer vision has been a personal interest since the start of my AI journey, and I understand the fundamentals of training vision models. That said, I don't have mission-critical CV projects in production yet. If your project needs production-grade computer vision, I recommend working with a dedicated CV specialist or talking to me about an experimental collaboration with clearly-scoped deliverables.
computerVision.anchorProject: Honest: no production CV projects of my own yet. For mission-critical CV I'll refer you to a dedicated specialist.

about.header: Alejandro Moyano Foncea · MSc Finance + MIT Prof Ed · 15 yrs running real ops

title.text: The person you'll actually work with.

about.bioParagraph1: I fix operational inefficiencies by building custom software. Unlike a traditional developer, I don't spend weeks writing code by hand — I use modern AI workflows on Claude Code to architect and ship production systems in a fraction of the usual time. My background isn't just tech, it's business: MSc in Finance, <mit>MIT Professional Education</mit> in AI/ML, and 15 years leading real operations before writing production code.
about.bioParagraph2: That's how I understand the *why* behind the software, not just the *how*. I've delivered everything from mining maintenance platforms for large-scale Chilean mining and custom ERPs for heavy industry, to engagement apps for education and decade-old legacy system modernization. I turn manual, paper-based work into digital tools that actually move the needle — no demos, no pitch decks, systems running today. US time zone overlap (0–2h from East/Central) and rates 3–5× below comparable SF/NYC shops, without giving up senior ownership.
about.italicQuote: I understand the why behind the software, not just the how.

contact.subtitle: I reply in under 24h. No endless forms.
